title: Regenerative Braking System with Power Monitor Display
design_question: How to implement regenerative braking energy recovery with real-time power monitoring?
selected_approach: BLDC hub motor (48 V, 500 W) operated in generator mode during braking: motor controller switches to regeneration mode via brake signal (Hall sensor on brake lever). Regenerated current charges Li-Ion battery pack via MPPT-style buck converter. INA226 power monitor (I2C, 0–36 V, 0–3.2 A) measures instantaneous and cumulative regenerated energy. SSD1306 OLED displays: regen power (W), energy recovered (Wh), state of charge. Arduino Mega orchestrates controller + display.
rationale: BLDC hub motors are designed for regeneration — no mechanical modifications needed. INA226 provides ±0.1% accuracy power measurement. OLED display gives immediate user feedback on energy savings. System recovers 10–30% of braking energy in urban cycling.
verification_notes: FACT: 'Regenerative Braking with BLDC Motor' confirms energy recovery 10–30% in urban stop-go conditions.
supporting_fact_titles: Regenerative Braking — BLDC Back-EMF Energy Recovery
CT Clamp Current Sensing and RMS Calculation
